_clinical_notes: CRITICAL red flags first: (1) Bladder/bowel dysfunction + saddle numbness = cauda equina = emergency. (2) Radiculopathy below knee with numbness/weakness = disc involvement = PT/physician referral. (3) High-velocity trauma in young athlete + severe pain = possible fracture (spondylolysis/spondylolisthesis in adolescents). Grade I-II muscle strain is the vast majority and is conservative. Mechanism: lifting+twisting most common. Assess: local vs. radiating, WB status, neuro symptoms.

red_flag_neuro.prompt: Do you have any of the following? (select the best match)

options.label: Loss of bladder or bowel control
Numbness in the groin or inner thighs

radiation.prompt: Does the pain travel down your leg?

options.label: No — stays in the lower back / buttock
Yes, into the buttock or upper thigh only
Yes, shoots below the knee — with tingling or numbness

mechanism.prompt: How did the back get injured?

options.label: Lifting something heavy or twisting suddenly

impact_back.prompt: After the impact, can you walk normally?

options.label: Cannot stand or walk / severe pain

weightbear_back.prompt: Can you stand upright and walk?

options.label: Yes, upright and walking normally (some pain)
Walking bent forward or with a limp
Cannot stand or walk due to pain

severity_back.prompt: How would you rate the pain at rest (not moving)?

options.label: Severe — very painful even at rest

outcome.seek_care_reason: Bladder/bowel changes or groin numbness after a back injury may indicate cauda equina syndrome — a medical emergency. Go to the ER now.
Pain shooting below the knee with numbness or tingling suggests nerve involvement (possible disc herniation). You should see a physician or PT for evaluation.
Severe back pain with inability to bear weight after trauma warrants imaging to rule out a vertebral fracture.
outcome.injury_subtype: Possible Vertebral Fracture / Serious Back Injury
Lower Back Muscle Strain - Grade I
Lower Back Muscle Strain - Grade II
Lower Back Muscle Strain - Grade II (High)
outcome.monitor_note: If pain is worsening or leg symptoms develop, seek evaluation within 48 hours.
